name: Home
title: Home
description: Landing page that loads IPTV service overview, key features, plans under a pricing threshold, featured series, a persistent global search bar, and an inline sign-in flow, using only the provided interfaces.

core_features: Load homepage content (service overview text, feature highlights, top plans under a pricing threshold, featured series, and authentication flags) using getHomePageContent when the page initializes.
Display a persistent global search bar in the header that sends users to search.html with a query URL parameter when they submit a search term.
Provide inline sign-in functionality using signInUser, updating the authenticated state and exposing a "Go to Dashboard" navigation path for authenticated users.
Show an overview of the IPTV service based on hero_title, hero_subtitle, and feature_highlights returned by getHomePageContent.
List top_plans_under_20 as a set of plan summaries that link users to the full plans.html page for detailed comparison and selection.
Highlight featured_series content (including documentary/nature series when present) and navigate to series.html with a seriesId URL parameter when a user selects a series.
Offer quick navigation entry points to plans.html, tv_guide.html, channels.html, and dashboard.html (for authenticated users) via clearly defined interactive elements.
user_workflows:
Anonymous user exploring plans:
1) User navigates to index.html.
2) homepage-data-loader component calls getHomePageContent.
3) Service overview section displays hero_title, hero_subtitle, and feature_highlights.
4) top-plans-list component displays the returned top_plans_under_20.
5) User clicks a "View Plans" CTA in either the overview section or near the plan list.
6) Browser navigates to plans.html (no additional URL parameters).

Anonymous user signing in and then going to dashboard:
1) User navigates to index.html.
2) homepage-data-loader calls getHomePageContent and receives is_authenticated = false.
3) sign-in-form-inline component is visible and displays email and password fields.
4) User fills email and password and submits the form.
5) sign-in-form-inline calls signInUser(email, password).
6) If signInUser returns success = false, the component shows the returned message as an error and keeps the form visible.
7) If signInUser returns success = true and is_authenticated = true, the component updates page state to is_authenticated = true and has_active_subscription according to the response.
8) The sign-in form hides; authenticated-cta-panel shows a "Go to Dashboard" button.
9) User clicks "Go to Dashboard".
10) Browser navigates to dashboard.html (no additional URL parameters).

Authenticated user using global search:
1) User is already authenticated (is_authenticated = true) when index.html loads.
2) homepage-data-loader calls getHomePageContent and receives is_authenticated = true.
3) header-global-navigation shows navigation links including a "Go to Dashboard" option and hides the inline sign-in form.
4) User types a search term (e.g., "nature") into the global search bar.
5) User submits the search form.
6) global-search-form constructs a URL search.html?query={encodedSearchTerm}.
7) Browser navigates to search.html with the query parameter, where search results are handled by another page.

User discovering a featured series from the homepage:
1) User navigates to index.html.
2) homepage-data-loader calls getHomePageContent and receives a featured_series array.
3) featured-series-grid displays a card for each Series item.
4) User clicks a featured series card.
5) featured-series-grid reads the selected Series.id as featuredSeriesId.
6) Browser navigates to series.html?seriesId={featuredSeriesId}.

User quickly accessing live TV or channel listings:
1) User navigates to index.html.
2) homepage-data-loader calls getHomePageContent.
3) quick-access-tiles component renders navigation tiles or links for "Watch Live TV", "Open TV Guide", and "Browse Channels".
4) User clicks the live TV guide-related element.
5) Browser navigates to tv_guide.html.
6) Alternatively, user clicks the channels-related element and the browser navigates to channels.html.
interactions:
Page initialization:
- Event: onInit of homepage-data-loader.
- Action: Call getHomePageContent with no parameters.
- Result: Store hero_title, hero_subtitle, feature_highlights, top_plans_under_20, featured_series, is_authenticated, and has_active_subscription in page state for binding to other components.

Global search input:
- Event: onChange in global-search-form search input.
- Action: Update local component state with the current text value (no API calls).
- Event: onSubmit in global-search-form.
- Condition: If the search term is non-empty, construct the URL search.html?query={encodeURIComponent(searchTerm)}.
- Action: Navigate browser to the constructed URL.

Clicking primary navigation links:
- Event: onClick on "Home" in header-global-navigation.
- Action: Navigate to index.html.
- Event: onClick on "Plans" link.
- Action: Navigate to plans.html.
- Event: onClick on "TV Guide" or equivalent live TV entry.
- Action: Navigate to tv_guide.html.
- Event: onClick on "Channels" link.
- Action: Navigate to channels.html.
- Event: onClick on "Go to Dashboard" (visible when is_authenticated = true).
- Action: Navigate to dashboard.html.

Submitting the inline sign-in form:
- Event: onSubmit in sign-in-form-inline.
- Precondition: User has entered email and password values.
- Action: Call signInUser(email, password).
- Intermediate State: sign-in-form-inline marks itself as loading (e.g., disables the submit button) while waiting for response.
- On Response (success = false):
  - Update an error message field with message from the response.
  - Keep is_authenticated unchanged.
  - Clear loading state so the user can retry.
- On Response (success = true and is_authenticated = true):
  - Update global page state: is_authenticated = true, has_active_subscription = response.has_active_subscription.
  - Hide the sign-in form component.
  - Show authenticated-cta-panel with a "Go to Dashboard" button.
- User may then click the button, triggering navigation to dashboard.html.

Interacting with top plans:
- Event: onClick on any plan card or "View Plans" CTA in top-plans-list or service-overview-section.
- Action: Navigate to plans.html (no plan-specific URL parameters on this page; detailed selection occurs there).

Interacting with featured series:
- Event: onClick on a featured series card in featured-series-grid.
- Action: Read the bound Series.id for that card as featuredSeriesId.
- Action: Navigate browser to series.html?seriesId={featuredSeriesId}.

Quick access to live TV and channels:
- Event: onClick on "Watch Live TV" or "Open TV Guide" tile in quick-access-tiles.
- Action: Navigate to tv_guide.html.
- Event: onClick on "Browse Channels" tile.
- Action: Navigate to channels.html.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The Home page does not read any incoming URL parameters. All functional state is derived from the getHomePageContent response and in-page interactions.

- Initial Data State:
  - On page load, homepage-data-loader invokes getHomePageContent.
  - The response populates a shared state object with: hero_title, hero_subtitle, feature_highlights, top_plans_under_20 (an array of Plan entities), featured_series (an array of Series entities), is_authenticated, and has_active_subscription.

- Authentication-Related State:
  - If is_authenticated = false on load:
    - sign-in-form-inline is visible.
    - authenticated-cta-panel is hidden.
    - header-global-navigation shows options appropriate for anonymous users (e.g., a sign-in trigger if needed).
  - If is_authenticated = true on load:
    - sign-in-form-inline is hidden.
    - authenticated-cta-panel is visible with a "Go to Dashboard" action.
    - header-global-navigation shows a "Go to Dashboard" link.
  - When sign-in-form-inline successfully calls signInUser and receives success = true and is_authenticated = true:
    - Page state is updated: is_authenticated = true and has_active_subscription = response.has_active_subscription.
    - This triggers conditional rendering changes: the sign-in form hides, authenticated-cta-panel and authenticated navigation options show.
    - No URL parameters are changed during sign-in; authentication persistence is handled outside this page's scope.

- Content State:
  - hero_title, hero_subtitle, and feature_highlights from getHomePageContent are passed directly to service-overview-section; no fallback or hardcoded content is used.
  - top_plans_under_20 is passed to top-plans-list; the component must handle empty arrays (e.g., show no plans list if none are returned) without introducing static data.
  - featured_series is passed to featured-series-grid; the component must also handle empty arrays (e.g., no featured series displayed) without static placeholders.

- Navigation State Via URL Parameters:
  - search.html: When the user submits the header search, the page constructs search.html?query={encodedSearchTerm}. The Home page does not parse this parameter; it only creates the URL.
  - series.html: When the user clicks a featured series, the page constructs series.html?seriesId={Series.id}. The Home page does not interpret seriesId; it only forwards the selected id.
  - plans.html, dashboard.html, tv_guide.html, channels.html: Navigation to these pages from the Home page does not append additional URL parameters, in accordance with the provided navigation rules.

- No Local Storage:
  - The Home page does not use localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies for functional state. All state is in memory for the lifetime of the page instance, with cross-page concerns delegated to the backend and the assigned interfaces.

components.functionality: On initial page load, call getHomePageContent to retrieve homepage marketing content, top plans, featured series, and authentication status, then expose this data to other components via a shared state or props system.

Provide persistent site-wide navigation links (Home, Plans, TV Guide, Channels, Dashboard when authenticated) and embed the global search form. Conditionally display sign-in or dashboard access options based on is_authenticated from homepage-data-loader or sign-in-form-inline.
Capture a free-text search query from the user in the header and navigate to search.html with the query as a URL parameter when submitted.
Display a concise overview of the IPTV service using hero_title, hero_subtitle, and feature_highlights from getHomePageContent, and provide a primary CTA that navigates users to plans.html.
Render the list of top plans under the specified pricing threshold using the top_plans_under_20 array from getHomePageContent. Each plan card can link to the plans.html page for more details.
Display featured on-demand series using the featured_series array returned by getHomePageContent and allow users to navigate to a series detail page by clicking a series card.
Provide quick functional entry points from the Home page to Watch Live TV/TV Guide, Browse Channels, and View Plans using simple interactive tiles or links.
Present email and password fields for the single user when is_authenticated = false. On submission, call signInUser and update authentication-related state and UI based on the response.
When is_authenticated = true, show a simple control that allows the user to proceed to the dashboard. This replaces the sign-in form for authenticated users on the Home page.

layout.grid_system: Visually consistent with a 12-column grid constrained to a centered max-width container for content; header and footer span full width with inner content aligned to this grid.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a linear, top-to-bottom structure anchored by a full-width, sticky dark header and a tall multi-column footer, with a single main content column centered in the viewport. Inside the main column, sections stack in this order: global search, service overview, top plans, featured series, quick access tiles, and inline sign-in. The data-loader appears as a light notification-style strip just under the header when active. For authenticated users, a floating CTA panel sits in the bottom-right, independent of the main flow, echoing the floating call button from the mockup.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a linear, top-to-bottom structure anchored by a full-width, sticky dark header and a tall multi-column footer, with a single main content column centered in the viewport. Inside the main column, sections stack in this order: global search, service overview, top plans, featured series, quick access tiles, and inline sign-in. The data-loader appears as a light notification-style strip just under the header when active. For authenticated users, a floating CTA panel sits in the bottom-right, independent of the main flow, echoing the floating call button from the mockup.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Header is a shallow band (~64–80px tall) spanning full width with its inner content constrained to a centered max-width container. The main content column occupies the middle half of the viewport height on load, with generous vertical gaps (32–48px) between sections and horizontal padding of ~64–80px inside the container. The footer occupies roughly the bottom third of the page, with clear multi-column structure and a thin copyright bar beneath. Main components generally span 8–12 of 12 columns within the container, while the floating CTA panel occupies a small fixed area at the bottom-right (~280px wide, ~1/5 of viewport width on desktop).
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain medium density overall. The header, search bar, and service overview are airy with generous whitespace and large type. Pricing plans and featured series are more content-dense but still well spaced with clear separation between cards/tiles. The footer is the densest area, with stacked link lists and compact text. No component should feel crowded; vertical spacing follows the 8px scale with 24–48px between major elements and 8–16px between related text lines.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye begins at the header logo (top-left) and the bright neon CTA (top-right), then tracks across the navigation. From there, it drops to the prominent, wide global search bar just below the header, then flows into the bold service overview heading and copy. Next, attention moves down to the pricing plans (key decision area), followed by the featured series grid and quick access tiles as discovery sections. Near the bottom, the inline sign-in form invites action for unauthenticated users. Finally, the gaze lands on the footer logo and link columns, with the floating authenticated CTA panel acting as a secondary attractor in the bottom-right for logged-in users.

layout.overall_description: The page sits on a deep black background with a centered max-width content container aligned to a 12-column grid. At the very top a full-width, sticky dark header bar runs edge to edge, with the IPTV logo on the left, compact navigation in the center, and a vivid neon-green primary CTA button anchored on the right. Immediately beneath the header, a wide global search bar spans most of the central column, visually framed by generous top and bottom padding so it reads as a persistent control. Below that, the content flows in a single, vertically stacked column: a left-aligned service overview headline with concise supporting text, a band of pricing plans, then a featured series grid, followed by compact quick-access tiles. Near the bottom of this main column sits the inline sign-in form, set apart with comfortable breathing room. The page then transitions into a tall, dark footer where the brand block occupies the leftmost area and three link columns ('Company', 'Product', 'Help') align to the right, all feeding into a slim copyright bar at the very bottom. Throughout, neon accents highlight key actions against the dark canvas, and for authenticated users, a small floating CTA panel appears in the bottom-right corner, hovering above the footer and main content as a modern, unobtrusive overlay.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader manifests as a horizontal notification-style strip directly beneath the header-global-navigation, aligned to the same centered max-width container. When active, it spans the full width of the main content column (approximately 80–90% of the viewport width on desktop) and sits flush against the bottom edge of the header, creating a clear, linear continuation of the top bar. Visually, it mimics the mockup’s light notification bar: a lighter, high-contrast band (charcoal or dark gray with a subtle lighter tint) on top of the black background, with a small loading spinner or status icon aligned to the left and status text to its right. The strip’s height is relatively shallow—about 48px with 12–16px vertical padding—keeping it unobtrusive and airy. When visible, it pushes all subsequent content (global-search-form and beyond) down uniformly, maintaining the vertical rhythm; when hidden, it collapses entirely so the global-search-form sits directly beneath the header.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears as a full-width, inline strip within the main flow, just under the sticky header. It does not scroll independently; it moves with the page content. It is conditionally rendered: present only during loading or status updates, and otherwise absent, causing no permanent reserved space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header-global-navigation forms a full-width dark band pinned to the very top of the page. Its background is pure or near-black, providing a strong base for neon accents. Inside, a centered max-width container adheres to a 12-column grid. The logo occupies the left portion (about 3 columns), sitting flush with a generous left padding of roughly 64–80px from the viewport edge. The main navigation links sit centered horizontally (about 5–6 middle columns), arranged as a single row of evenly spaced text links. On the far right (3–4 columns), a bright neon-green primary CTA button—such as 'Get Started' or 'Start Watching'—anchors the bar, aligned to the right edge of the grid with matching right padding (64–80px). The header’s height is shallow and sleek, approximately 64–72px on desktop, maintaining a low profile while clearly separating it from the main content. Elements are vertically centered within this bar, with minimal vertical padding and no visible shadows, only subtle corner rounding on the CTA button.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sticky at the top of the viewport. As the user scrolls, the header remains fixed, maintaining global navigation and the primary CTA in constant view. Inner content remains centered and responsive within the max-width container, scaling down to a collapsed layout (logo left, hamburger right) on smaller screens while preserving the same top anchoring behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global-search-form sits directly below the header (or the homepage-data-loader when active), occupying a prominent position at the top of the main content column. It is horizontally centered within the same max-width container and spans roughly 8–10 of the 12 grid columns, resulting in a search bar that covers about 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop. There is a comfortable vertical gap of about 32px from the bottom of the header/data-loader and another 32–40px before the service-overview-section, reinforcing its importance and giving it a clean, standalone appearance. Visually, the search field is a long, pill-shaped or slightly rounded rectangle with a dark gray fill against the black background, outlined or accented with a thin neon-green stroke or focused glow. A search icon aligns on the left inside the field, with the input text centered or slightly left-aligned, and a subtle placeholder like 'Search channels, series, or sports'. The form height is around 48–56px, creating a substantial but not bulky control that feels persistent and central to the experience.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Initially positioned inline within the main flow, but becomes sticky just below the header upon scrolling. As the user scrolls past its original position, the search bar pins to the top of the viewport, directly under the sticky header, maintaining its width and alignment. This gives a stacked fixed-top experience: header at the very top, then the search bar right below, while the rest of the content scrolls underneath.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The service-overview-section sits immediately beneath the global-search-form, acting as the primary hero-like content block for the homepage. It is aligned to the same centered max-width container and spans nearly the full 12-column width on desktop, with the core text content occupying the left 6–8 columns for a clean, left-aligned composition. A bold, large headline ('IPTV streaming without the hassle' or similar) appears at the top-left of this block, with 8–16px spacing to a concise subheading and 8–12px between subsequent bullet points or key highlights. The right side of the grid (remaining 4–6 columns) can remain largely open or host a minimal abstract illustration or iconography, but maintains the mockup’s restrained, flat aesthetic—no heavy imagery, just subtle visuals if needed. Vertical padding within the section is generous—about 48–64px top and bottom—creating an airy, low-density feel consistent with a hero area. The background remains the same dark base as the page, with perhaps a very subtle gradient or horizontal line to distinguish the block without introducing strong panels.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content flow. It expands in height as needed for copy but is capped with consistent vertical padding to maintain a spacious hero feel. On smaller screens, the content collapses to a purely single-column layout, with text centered or left-aligned and any illustrative elements stacked below.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The top-plans-list section appears directly below the service-overview-section, separated by a vertical gap of about 32–40px. It remains within the same centered max-width container. At the top-left of this section, a clear section title such as 'Top Plans Under $X' or 'Best Value Plans' anchors the content, aligned to the left and spanning roughly 6–8 columns. Below the heading, the plans are presented as a row of 2–3 horizontally arranged plan cards on desktop, each card aligned within the 12-column grid (e.g., three cards of 4 columns each with 16–24px gutters). Each card has a dark surface slightly lighter than the page background, subtle 8px corner rounding, and neon-green accents for price and action buttons. The cards are vertically balanced with 16–24px padding inside and 8–12px between text lines, keeping content readable but compact. The overall section height is moderate—roughly 1/3 of the viewport height on a typical desktop—so it feels substantial yet not overwhelming. A bit more vertical spacing (24–32px) separates the heading from the cards to mimic the airy elements from the mockup while increasing density slightly within the cards themselves.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, stacked within the main column. The layout is responsive: on medium screens, cards may drop to two per row; on small screens, each plan card becomes full-width in a vertical stack, preserving the same internal padding and spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured-series-grid is positioned beneath the top-plans-list, with a vertical separation of about 40px to clearly signal a new content area. It shares the same centered max-width container. A left-aligned section title ('Featured Series') anchors the top of the block, with 16–24px of space before the grid itself. The grid then displays thumbnail tiles for series in a multi-column format: on desktop, 3–4 tiles per row depending on the chosen card width (e.g., three tiles at 4 columns each or four tiles at 3 columns each). Each tile is a vertically oriented rectangle with a series image or stylized cover, a dark overlay or subtle gradient, and title text beneath or overlaid at the bottom. Spacing between tiles is around 16–24px horizontally and vertically, making this section somewhat more dense than the hero and pricing but still orderly. The background remains the page’s dark base, so the series art and neon accent focus states (e.g., a thin neon border on hover) provide the primary visual variation. This grid can extend down for multiple rows, occupying up to 1/3–1/2 of the viewport height depending on content volume.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, flowing with the rest of the page. It grows vertically as more series are featured. On smaller screens, the grid collapses to 2 tiles per row and eventually to a single-column list, while preserving consistent gutters for breathing room.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quick-access-tiles section sits below the featured-series-grid, separated by roughly 32–40px of vertical space. Within the same max-width container, this block offers a set of small navigation tiles for key areas such as 'Live Sports', 'Movies', 'Support', or 'Apps'. The section may begin with a subtle, smaller heading like 'Quick Access' aligned left, followed by a grid of tiles arranged in 2–3 columns on desktop (e.g., four tiles as two columns by two rows, each tile spanning 4–6 columns). Each tile is a compact rectangular button with a dark background distinct from the page base, slight corner rounding, and neon-green accents (icon, border, or text). The tiles are sized to be visually lighter and less tall than plan cards, with about 16–20px internal padding and 16–24px gaps between tiles. Overall, the section height is modest, creating a dense but not crowded cluster of navigational shortcuts and acting as a bridge between content discovery (featured-series-grid) and account actions (sign-in-form-inline).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and responsive; tile count per row adapts to available width (3 per row on large screens, 2 on medium, 1 on small). The section maintains its relative position above the sign-in area regardless of screen size.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sign-in-form-inline is located near the bottom of the main content column, following the quick-access-tiles with a vertical gap of about 40–48px to clearly distinguish it as a separate, action-oriented area. It is horizontally centered within the max-width container but does not stretch edge to edge; instead, the form body occupies roughly 4–6 of the 12 columns (about 40–50% of the container width), creating a focused, compact panel either centered or slightly left-biased. A clear heading such as 'Sign in to start watching' appears at the top, left-aligned, with 8–16px spacing to a short helper line if needed. Below, the input fields (email, password, and any others) are stacked vertically with 12–16px between them, each using the same dark form styling as the global search (rounded rectangles, dark gray fill, subtle neon accent on focus). A prominent neon-green 'Sign In' button spans the width of the form, with 12–16px vertical padding and a slightly larger height than the inputs to emphasize action. The entire block sits on the same background as the rest of the page, relying on spacing and alignment rather than heavy panel styling to stand out. Vertical padding above and below the form content is generous (32–40px) to give it a calm, intentional presence before the footer.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main flow, but conditionally visible depending on authentication state. When the user is signed in, this form can collapse or be replaced inline by a brief status message, reducing visual weight while preserving the overall vertical rhythm.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The authenticated-cta-panel appears only for logged-in users and is positioned as a floating overlay element in the bottom-right corner of the viewport, visually inspired by the mockup’s floating call button. It does not occupy space in the main content column. Instead, it hovers above the page content with a fixed distance of about 24–32px from both the right and bottom edges of the viewport. The panel is a compact, horizontally oriented dark rectangle with subtle 8–12px corner rounding, approximately 260–320px wide and 100–140px tall on desktop. Inside, a short, bold line such as 'Resume Watching' or 'Go to Your Dashboard' aligns to the left, with smaller supporting text beneath. To the right or along the bottom of the panel sits a bright neon-green CTA button, visually similar to the header CTA but scaled down, encouraging a quick, high-value action. The background of the panel is a slightly lighter black/charcoal than the main page, and it may have a very subtle border or glow to distinguish it as floating, but no heavy shadows. It remains visible as the user scrolls, visually overlaying the lower portion of content or the top of the footer, acting as a persistent but unobtrusive prompt.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position overlay anchored to the bottom-right of the viewport, independent of page scroll. It maintains its relative offset from the edges across screen sizes, and on smaller devices may reduce in width or collapse into a more FAB-like pill to avoid covering core content. It appears only when the user is authenticated, effectively taking over the role of the inline sign-in-form-inline as the primary account-related action point.

name: Dashboard
title: Dashboard
description: Signed-in dashboard that summarizes the user’s current IPTV subscription, usage indicators, and upcoming recordings, and provides quick access to plan, billing, settings, favorites, and TV guide pages.

core_features: Load and display the current subscription summary (current base plan, active add-ons, simultaneous streams limit, total monthly and annual prices) using the getDashboardOverview interface.
Show key usage indicators (favorite channels count, scheduled recordings count, upcoming recordings count) using getDashboardOverview.usage_indicators.
List upcoming recordings, including recording status and associated channel name, using getDashboardOverview.upcoming_recordings.
Provide a direct link from the current plan summary to my_plan.html with the appropriate navigation parameter {from: 'dashboard'}.
Provide a direct link from the plan area to plans.html with the appropriate navigation parameter {context: 'change_plan'} to change or manage the plan.
Provide a shortcut to billing.html from the dashboard with the navigation parameter {from: 'dashboard'}.
Provide a shortcut to settings.html (parental controls section) via the header/profile menu with the navigation parameter {section: 'parental_controls'}.
Provide quick links to tv_guide.html (from the dashboard or from individual upcoming recording items).
Provide quick access to favorites.html (e.g., via a 'My Channels' or 'Favorites' shortcut on the dashboard).

Display contextual status messages on the dashboard using the incoming URL parameter from (e.g., 'plan_upgrade', 'billing_change', 'parental_controls_updated', or others) to confirm the result of the user’s last action.
user_workflows: Viewing current subscription details: 1) User navigates to dashboard.html (either directly or after sign-in / another flow). 2) On page load, the Dashboard controller component calls getDashboardOverview with no parameters. 3) When the response is received, the subscription_summary data is passed to the Plan Summary component. 4) The Plan Summary component displays the current_plan name, key attributes (such as channel_count and max_simultaneous_streams), the subscription.billing_cycle, simultaneous_streams_limit, and total_monthly_price / total_annual_price. 5) The user can click 'View Plan Details' to go to my_plan.html?from=dashboard or 'Change Plan' to go to plans.html?context=change_plan.

Reviewing billing information: 1) User opens dashboard.html. 2) Dashboard loads getDashboardOverview (for context) and displays a Billing shortcut card (using subscription_summary totals, and optionally placeholders where more detail is shown on the Billing page). 3) The user clicks the Billing shortcut or link. 4) Browser navigates to billing.html?from=dashboard, where detailed billing settings are managed on that page.

Checking usage indicators (favorites and recordings): 1) User opens dashboard.html. 2) After getDashboardOverview resolves, the Usage Indicators component reads usage_indicators.favorite_channels_count, usage_indicators.scheduled_recordings_count, and usage_indicators.upcoming_recordings_count. 3) The component displays each count and context labels. 4) The user can click a 'View Favorites' action (if present within this widget or a linked tile) which navigates to favorites.html. 5) The user can click a 'Manage Recordings' or similar action that navigates to tv_guide.html (or another recordings-specific page if added later; for now only tv_guide.html is defined).

Managing upcoming recordings: 1) User opens dashboard.html. 2) The Upcoming Recordings List component receives getDashboardOverview.upcoming_recordings. 3) It lists each upcoming recording item with title, channel_name, start_time/end_time (derived from the Recording model fields). 4) The user clicks a 'View in TV Guide' or 'See all' link in the upcoming recordings section. 5) Browser navigates to tv_guide.html with no additional parameters, where more recording options can be managed.

Accessing parental controls from the dashboard: 1) User opens dashboard.html. 2) The header/profile menu component is rendered. 3) The user opens the profile menu and selects a 'Settings' option specifically for parental controls. 4) Browser navigates to settings.html?section=parental_controls. 5) After updating settings on settings.html, that page may redirect back to dashboard.html?from=parental_controls_updated, which the dashboard uses to show a contextual confirmation message.

Returning to the dashboard after subscription or billing changes: 1) User changes plan or add-ons via plans.html and confirms via checkout.html, which then redirects to dashboard.html?from=plan_upgrade (or a similar value in the from parameter). 2) On load, the Dashboard controller reads the from URL parameter. 3) The Status Message Banner component maps this parameter to a message key such as 'plan_upgrade_success'. 4) The banner shows a non-invasive confirmation message at the top of the dashboard. 5) The user reviews updated subscription_summary and can continue browsing or navigate elsewhere.

Navigating to favorites from dashboard shortcuts: 1) User opens dashboard.html. 2) The Usage Indicators or a dedicated Favorites Shortcut Tile component shows the favorite_channels_count from usage_indicators. 3) The user clicks this tile or link. 4) Browser navigates to favorites.html (no extra parameters). 5) After managing favorites, the user can return to dashboard.html from the main header navigation.
interactions: Page load and data fetching: On initial load of dashboard.html, a controller component reads the from URL parameter if present, then triggers getDashboardOverview(). When the promise resolves, it distributes subscription_summary, usage_indicators, and upcoming_recordings to downstream components via props or a shared state mechanism.

Status message display: If a from URL parameter exists, the Status Banner component uses its value to select a result-type (e.g., success, info, error) and a message key. The component then renders a contextual notice (for example, after 'plan_upgrade' or 'billing_change'). The user can dismiss the banner via a close button (onClick handler), which hides the message for the current session without using localStorage.
View Plan Details navigation: In the Plan Summary card, the user can click a 'View Plan Details' button. The onClick handler triggers navigation to my_plan.html?from=dashboard.
Change/Manage Plan navigation: In the Plan Summary card, the user can click a 'Change Plan' or 'Manage Plan' link. The onClick handler triggers navigation to plans.html?context=change_plan.
Billing navigation: In a Billing shortcut tile, the user can click a primary action (e.g., 'Manage Billing'). The onClick handler navigates to billing.html?from=dashboard.

Settings (Parental Controls) navigation: In the header/profile menu, the user chooses a 'Settings' option associated with parental controls. The onClick handler navigates to settings.html?section=parental_controls.

TV Guide navigation from header or quick link: The dashboard may include a 'TV Guide' quick link or button. When clicked, the onClick handler navigates to tv_guide.html with no extra query parameters.

TV Guide navigation from upcoming recordings: For each item in the Upcoming Recordings List, a 'View in TV Guide' link is available. The onClick handler navigates to tv_guide.html. Any additional context such as channel or time is handled internally by tv_guide.html (no parameters are passed according to the outgoing connection definition).
Favorites navigation: A Favorites shortcut tile or link (e.g., 'My Channels' or 'Favorites') is clickable. The onClick handler navigates to favorites.html.

Internal display toggles (non-persistent): Within the Plan Summary card, the user may toggle between emphasizing monthly and annual pricing views. These toggles are handled entirely in client-side state for the current render (e.g., a boolean state 'showAnnual'), without affecting URL parameters or calling additional interfaces.

Handling API loading and error states: While getDashboardOverview is in progress, the Dashboard controller sets a 'loading' state. Components that depend on data display a loading indicator state (e.g., skeletons or simple 'Loading...' text). If getDashboardOverview fails, an error state is set, and an error message is shown via the Status Banner component with an appropriate message key; components avoid rendering incomplete data until a successful response is available.
page_functionality.state_logic: The main URL parameter affecting dashboard behavior is 'from'. On page initialization, the Dashboard controller parses window.location.search to read 'from'. If 'from' is present, it is passed to the Status Message Banner component as a string value. The banner maps known values (e.g., 'plan_upgrade', 'billing_change', 'parental_controls_updated', 'signin') to message keys and severity levels (e.g., success, info). Unknown or unsupported 'from' values may be ignored or mapped to a generic info message. After this, the controller calls getDashboardOverview(). No state is stored in localStorage; all transient UI state (such as loading flags, selected pricing view, dismissed status messages) is held in memory only. Outgoing navigation from the dashboard always sets explicit URL parameters as defined: my_plan.html?from=dashboard when the user chooses to view plan details; plans.html?context=change_plan when managing the plan; billing.html?from=dashboard from billing shortcuts; settings.html?section=parental_controls from the profile/settings interaction; tv_guide.html from TV Guide links with no added parameters; favorites.html from favorites links with no added parameters. Whenever a user returns to the dashboard from other pages, those pages can send back context with the 'from' query parameter, which the dashboard uses to display the corresponding status message. The dashboard does not define or depend on any additional URL parameters beyond 'from' for its own behavior.

components.functionality: Central controller that initializes the dashboard, parses URL parameters, calls getDashboardOverview, manages loading and error states, and distributes data to child components.
Displays contextual success or information messages at the top of the dashboard based on the 'from' URL parameter or API error states.

Shows current base plan details, billing cycle, simultaneous streams limit, and total monthly/annual subscription price. Provides navigation actions to view detailed plan information or change the plan.
Lists currently active add-ons associated with the subscription, highlighting their categories and summarizing their contribution to the plan (purely informational on this page).

Displays high-level usage metrics such as favorite channels count, scheduled recordings count, and upcoming recordings count. Provides navigation shortcuts to favorites and TV guide where appropriate.
Shows a list of upcoming recordings with title, channel name, and scheduled time. Provides navigation to the TV guide for further recording management.
Provides a concise entry point to billing management, using subscription summary data to indicate the current recurring amount and linking to billing.html with appropriate parameters.
Provides quick access to the user's favorite channels page, optionally displaying the current favorite_channels_count.
Provides a direct link from the dashboard to the TV guide for browsing channels and scheduling recordings.
Top-level navigation bar for signed-in users, with a Dashboard item, a profile menu, and a Settings (parental controls) link that passes the correct URL parameter.

layout.grid_system: Visually consistent with a 12-column grid constrained to a centered max-width container for content; header and footer span full width with inner content aligned to this grid.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a full-width dark page with a fixed/sticky global header at the top, a single main content column centered within a max-width container, and a tall multi-column footer at the bottom (part of the overall site shell). Inside the main content, components follow a top-to-bottom flow: header navigation, a prominent 'Dashboard' heading with the dashboard controller aligned to its right, then a full-width status banner, followed by stacked summary and activity cards (plan, usage, addons, recordings) and finally a row of navigation shortcuts near the bottom. This preserves the simple, linear reading order observed in the mockup while accommodating more dashboard content.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a full-width dark page with a fixed/sticky global header at the top, a single main content column centered within a max-width container, and a tall multi-column footer at the bottom (part of the overall site shell). Inside the main content, components follow a top-to-bottom flow: header navigation, a prominent 'Dashboard' heading with the dashboard controller aligned to its right, then a full-width status banner, followed by stacked summary and activity cards (plan, usage, addons, recordings) and finally a row of navigation shortcuts near the bottom. This preserves the simple, linear reading order observed in the mockup while accommodating more dashboard content.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies a shallow band at the top (roughly 64–80px high). The main dashboard content sits within a centered container constrained to a 12-column grid, with generous horizontal padding from the viewport edges (~64–80px on large screens). Vertically, sections are separated by 24–48px gaps matching the mockup’s spacing scale: tighter gaps (16–24px) between closely related items, and larger breaks (32–48px) between major groups. The footer consumes the bottom visual third of the page, echoing the dense multi-column feel from the mockup. Within the main area, the plan and usage components take more vertical space, while addons and shortcuts occupy comparatively smaller blocks and rows.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain medium density overall. The area around the heading, controller, and status banner remains airy, with ample dark background visible between elements. Summary and usage cards are more information-dense but still organized with comfortable line spacing and clear separation between sections inside each card. The upcoming recordings list becomes the most content-dense section, similar in feel to the footer columns in the mockup, though still legible and well-spaced. The navigation shortcuts are compact but not cramped, reflecting the mockup’s approach of clearer breathing room around key actions.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts in the header with the brand logo on the left and a bright neon CTA on the right, then transitions down to the large 'Dashboard' heading. From there, the eye is led immediately to the high-contrast status message banner (similar to the cart notification in the mockup). After acknowledging the status, the user’s gaze flows downward through the plan summary, usage and addons row, and the upcoming recordings list. The bottom row of shortcut tiles acts as a closing set of actions before the footer, while a floating support or call button anchored to the bottom-right (outside this component list but part of the overall shell) provides a secondary, persistent attractor at the edge of the flow.

layout.overall_description: The page sits on a deep black background with a fixed dark header spanning the full width at the top. Inside this header, content is aligned to a centered max-width grid: the logo anchors the left, navigation links sit in the middle, and a neon green CTA button glows on the right, all in a slim horizontal band. Below the header, the main dashboard content feels like a single tall column centered in the viewport, starting with a large, left-aligned 'Dashboard' title. On the same horizontal line, a compact dashboard controller aligns to the right, visually balancing the title without overpowering it. A light-toned status message banner stretches across the full width of this content column just below, contrasting sharply against the dark page and immediately communicating account state or alerts. Further down, key information is presented in stacked cards: a plan summary card spans the main column, followed by a row where usage indicators dominate the left two-thirds while active addons occupy the right third, still framed as one visual band. Beneath that, a full-width upcoming recordings list becomes the most detailed panel, anchoring the central content area. Near the bottom of the viewport, three shortcut tiles—Billing, Favorites, and TV Guide—sit side by side in a horizontal row, offering quick navigation in a compact, high-contrast strip above the dense, multi-column footer that fills the lower portion of the page. A floating, circular neon action button hovers at the bottom-right corner of the screen, independent of the scrolling content, echoing the site’s techy, streaming-focused aesthetic and acting as a persistent support or call-to-action affordance.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header-navigation forms a full-width horizontal bar pinned to the very top of the viewport on a solid black background. Inside, content is constrained to a centered max-width container aligned to the 12-column grid, with approximately 64–80px horizontal padding from the viewport edges. The brand logo occupies the left-most 2–3 columns, aligned vertically to the middle of the bar. Main navigation links are centered in the header, distributed across the middle 6–8 columns as a horizontal list with even spacing. On the right-most 2–3 columns, a bright neon green CTA button (for example, 'Start Watching' or 'Upgrade') sits flush to the right edge of the grid, slightly more prominent than other elements. The header is relatively shallow—about 64–72px tall—mirroring the mockup’s compact top bar. Below the header, a consistent vertical gap of about 32px separates it from the main dashboard heading.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed/sticky at the top of the viewport, spanning full width. Inner content remains centered and responsive: on narrower screens, navigation links collapse and stack or become a menu, while logo and CTA remain anchored left/right within the grid.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dashboard-controller sits in the main content column in the first content row beneath the header, sharing a horizontal line with the page title. The overall row spans the full width of the inner container (12 columns). The 'Dashboard' heading occupies the left 6–8 columns, while the dashboard-controller is right-aligned in the remaining 4–6 columns, tucked against the right margin of the grid. This controller might appear as a compact segmented control, filter dropdowns, or quick toggles in a pill-shaped or subtle outline style. Vertically, it aligns with the baseline of the heading text to form a balanced row. A vertical gap of about 24px is left below this row before the status-message-banner. On smaller screens, the controller wraps beneath the heading and expands to nearly full width, but always remains visually grouped with the title as the immediate secondary control element.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the regular scrolling content. It stays anchored to the right side of the main content row on larger screens and reflows beneath the title in a stacked arrangement on smaller viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The status-message-banner appears immediately below the title/controller row, spanning the full width of the main content container across all 12 columns. It takes the form of a horizontal strip with a lighter, high-contrast background (dark grey or desaturated tone) against the black page, echoing the notification strip from the cart mockup. The banner has subtle rounded corners and padding of about 12–16px vertically and 16–24px horizontally. Inside, an icon or status indicator sits flush to the left, followed by the status text that stretches toward the right. The content is left-aligned, with comfortable line spacing and no crowding. There is a generous vertical gap of around 24–32px above and below the banner, ensuring it reads as a distinct, important message before the main dashboard cards.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows with the main content, always occupying the full width of the content column. On smaller screens, text may wrap to multiple lines but the banner remains a single, easily tappable strip.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The plan-summary-card is the first substantive card below the status-message-banner and anchors the top of the main dashboard data. It is placed centrally within the main content column, spanning all 12 grid columns on large screens but with internal padding that keeps content comfortably within an 8–10 column visual width. The card has a dark charcoal background distinct from the page black, with subtle rounding and no visible shadow. Its vertical size is moderate to tall, occupying roughly one screen height on smaller laptops when combined with the surrounding spacing. At the top-left of the card, a smaller section title like 'Current Plan' is aligned, with key plan details (plan name, renewal date, price, number of connections, etc.) arranged in two to three columns inside the card, but still reading left-to-right as a single block. Vertical spacing of 16–24px separates sections within the card, and a 32px gap separates this card from the row below (usage and addons). The card sits clearly in the linear flow, feeling like the primary summary block for the account.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level behavior in the flow, full-width within the content container. On narrower screens, inner columns collapse into a single stacked column while maintaining comfortable vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The usage-indicators-widget is placed directly below the plan-summary-card in a shared horizontal row that visually pairs it with the active-addons-list. This row spans the 12-column grid. The usage-indicators-widget claims the left two-thirds of the row—approximately 8 of the 12 columns—making it the dominant element within this band. It has the same dark card background and rounded style as the plan summary, but slightly shorter in height. Inside, one to three key usage metrics (e.g., bandwidth, streaming hours, DVR space) are arranged in a horizontal layout of compact gauges or bar indicators, centered vertically within the card. There is about 24px of vertical gap above this row and about 24–32px below before the upcoming recordings list. The positioning emphasizes that usage is a major dashboard element without breaking the overall top-to-bottom reading pattern.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Shares a row with active-addons-list on larger screens, sitting in the left 8 columns. On smaller screens, it becomes a full-width card and stacks above the addons list, preserving the vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The active-addons-list sits in the same horizontal row as the usage-indicators-widget, positioned on the right side of the main content column. It occupies roughly the rightmost 4 of the 12 grid columns, resulting in a narrower, vertical card that visually complements the broader usage widget. The card height is similar or slightly shorter than the usage widget so the two align along their top edges and nearly align along their bottom edges, forming a clean horizontal band. Within the card, addon entries are stacked vertically with 8–12px between each line, reflecting a medium density that stays readable. If there are few addons, the card keeps enough vertical padding (16–24px) to avoid feeling empty. A 24–32px vertical gap separates this shared row from the upcoming recordings section. The layout makes the addons card feel related to usage but clearly secondary in importance due to its reduced width and content mass.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On large screens, the card is right-aligned in the row, occupying the last 4 columns. On smaller screens, it breaks out of this two-column row and stacks beneath the usage widget as a full-width card, keeping consistent margins and spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The upcoming-recordings-list is positioned below the usage/addons row as a full-width card within the main content column, spanning all 12 grid columns. It is the tallest content block in the central section of the dashboard, designed to hold a scrollable or vertically stacked list of upcoming programs, each row showing title, channel, time, and possibly status or actions. The card shares the same dark tone and subtle rounding as the other cards but appears more content-dense, similar to the footer’s multi-column layout in the mockup. Individual recording rows have 8–12px vertical padding and are separated by subtle dividers or spacing, creating a clear hierarchy without clutter. The card sits roughly in the middle of the page height when the user scrolls slightly, forming a strong visual anchor. Above it there is a 24–32px gap from the usage/addons row, and below it a 32–40px gap before the navigation shortcut tiles.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard scrollable content. If the list grows long, the card scrolls with the page; on smaller screens, the list may truncate with a 'View all' link at the bottom to maintain reasonable height while preserving the same full-width presence.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The billing-shortcut-card appears near the bottom of the main content area as part of a horizontal row of navigation shortcuts. This row sits above the site footer and below the upcoming recordings card, separated by approximately 32–40px of vertical space. Within the 12-column grid, the billing-shortcut-card occupies the leftmost 4 columns. It appears as a compact but slightly taller tile or card, with a dark background and neon-accented icon or border to signal its importance. The tile includes a concise label like 'Billing & Payments' and possibly one line of helper text. All three shortcut tiles in the row (billing, favorites, TV guide) are aligned along their top and bottom edges, with 16–24px horizontal gaps between them, creating a balanced strip of actions. The billing tile is subtly emphasized by either a slightly stronger neon accent or marginally larger height, indicating it is the primary shortcut of the group.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of a three-tile row in the normal flow. On smaller screens, it becomes the first tile in a vertical stack of shortcuts, each occupying the full width of the content column with consistent spacing between them.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The favorites-shortcut-tile sits in the same horizontal shortcut row as the billing and TV guide cards, centered within the main content column. It occupies the middle 4 of the 12 grid columns, directly to the right of the billing-shortcut-card and to the left of the TV guide tile, with equal horizontal spacing (16–24px) between neighbors. The tile is a compact rectangle with the shared dark card styling, containing a clear label such as 'Favorites' and optionally a small icon suggesting saved shows or channels. Its height matches the TV guide tile and is very close to the billing tile’s height to maintain a clean horizontal band. This placement positions Favorites as a convenient, but slightly less critical, navigation item compared to Billing, while still making it easy to scan and access in the closing section of the dashboard.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows as the middle tile in a three-column row on larger screens. On smaller viewports, it stacks directly beneath the billing shortcut card, full-width, maintaining consistent vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The tv-guide-shortcut-tile completes the shortcut row at the bottom of the main content, occupying the rightmost 4 of the 12 grid columns. Aligned with the billing and favorites tiles, it forms the right edge of the row, with its right side roughly lining up with the right edge of the main content container. The tile’s styling matches the others: dark background, subtle rounding, and a neon-accented icon or border. The label, such as 'TV Guide', is left-aligned within the tile with 16–24px horizontal padding. This tile may include a small status line like 'Now: Live' or 'Primetime lineup' but remains concise. A 32–40px vertical gap separates this entire shortcut row from the start of the footer, keeping it visually distinct as the final interactive section of the dashboard content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears as the right-hand tile in the shortcut row on larger screens, and as the last tile in a vertically stacked series on smaller screens, preserving consistent margins and vertical spacing.

name: Plans
title: Plans
description: Allows users to browse, filter, sort, compare, and select IPTV subscription plans for new subscriptions or plan changes, with all state driven by URL parameters and backend interfaces.

core_features: Load available plan filter and sort options using getPlanFilterOptions and render dynamic controls for maximum monthly price, minimum channel count, billing cycle, and sort order.
List plans using getPlansForListing with filters, sort, and context derived from URL parameters and user interactions, highlighting the current plan when in a change-plan context.
Allow users to adjust filters (max monthly price, minimum channel count, billing cycle) and sort order, updating the URL query parameters and reloading the plan list via getPlansForListing.

Enable selecting a plan for a new subscription or change-plan flow by calling startPlanChange with the selected plan, billing cycle, and changeContext, then navigating to my_plan.html for change preview.
Provide checkboxes on plan cards to select plans for comparison, synchronize selected plan IDs via URL query parameters, and display a side-by-side comparison using comparePlans.
Maintain page state (context, billing cycle filter, filters, sort option, selected comparison plans) exclusively via URL parameters so that the page can be reloaded or shared with identical state.
user_workflows:
Workflow: New subscription plan selection
1) User navigates to plans.html with context set to a new-subscription-related value in the URL (e.g., context=new_subscription).
2) On page init, the page reads context and optional billingCycle from the URL.
3) The page calls getPlanFilterOptions to populate filter controls.
4) Using the URL parameters (maxMonthlyPrice, minChannelCount, billingCycle, sort) and the context, the page calls getPlansForListing.
5) The page displays the resulting plans in the list, with filters and sort controls reflecting the applied_filters returned by getPlansForListing.
6) The user adjusts filters and sort as needed; each change updates the URL query parameters and triggers a new call to getPlansForListing.
7) When the user clicks "Select Plan" on a plan card, the page calls startPlanChange with planId, billingCycle (from the current filter or selection), and changeContext derived from context.
8) If startPlanChange returns success, the page navigates to my_plan.html with parameters {from: 'plans', changePreview: 'true'} so that the user can review the plan summary and confirm subscription.

Workflow: Change plan from dashboard or billing
1) User navigates from dashboard.html or billing.html to plans.html with context set to a change-plan-related value (e.g., context=change_plan) and optionally billingCycle.
2) On page init, the page reads context and billingCycle and calls getPlanFilterOptions.
3) The billingCycle URL value, if provided, is used as the default billing cycle filter; otherwise, the page falls back to existing URL filter or applied_filters from getPlansForListing.
4) The page calls getPlansForListing with filters, sort, and context; the response includes current_plan_id used to mark the active subscription plan.
5) The user can filter and sort plans; the URL is updated with each change and the listing is reloaded via getPlansForListing.
6) The user may select multiple plans for comparison via checkboxes; selected plan IDs are stored in a URL parameter and reflected in the comparison selector.
7) When the user clicks "Select Plan" on a different plan, the page calls startPlanChange with planId, the chosen billingCycle, and changeContext based on context.
8) On success, the page navigates to my_plan.html with {from: 'plans', changePreview: 'true'} for review of the pending change.

Workflow: Plan comparison
1) User lands on plans.html with any context, and the page loads filters and plans via getPlanFilterOptions and getPlansForListing.
2) For each listed plan, the user can toggle a comparison checkbox.
3) When a checkbox is toggled, the selected plan IDs are written to a URL parameter (e.g., compare=planId1,planId2,...).
4) The comparison selector component reads the selected IDs from the URL and displays the number of selected plans and an action to open comparison.
5) When the user clicks the "Compare" action, the page calls comparePlans with the list of selected plan IDs read from the URL.
6) The comparison modal or in-page comparison view displays side-by-side details of the returned Plan objects, including pricing and max_simultaneous_streams.
7) Closing the comparison view does not modify the URL; clearing selected comparisons updates the comparison URL parameter to an empty or absent state so that reloading the page resets comparison selection.

Workflow: Sharing or reloading filtered plans view
1) User arrives at plans.html, adjusts filters (maxMonthlyPrice, minChannelCount, billingCycle) and sort.
2) Each adjustment updates the URL query parameters (for example: ?context=change_plan&billingCycle=monthly&maxMonthlyPrice=...&minChannelCount=...&sort=...).
3) The same URL can be reloaded or shared; on load, the page reads all query parameters and reconstructs the filter and sort state without using local storage.
4) The page calls getPlansForListing with filters and sort derived from the URL and shows a view consistent with the saved URL state.
interactions:
Interaction: Initial page load
- Event: onInit for the page.
- Action: Read URL parameters (context, billingCycle, maxMonthlyPrice, minChannelCount, sort, compare).
- Call getPlanFilterOptions to load allowable ranges and options.
- Compute effective filters from URL parameters and getPlanFilterOptions (e.g., clamp maxMonthlyPrice to allowed range if necessary).
- Call getPlansForListing with {filters, sort, context} and render results.
- If a compare parameter is present, keep those plan IDs selected in the UI.

Interaction: Change max monthly price filter
- Event: onChange on the max monthly price control (e.g., slider or input).
- Action: Update maxMonthlyPrice in the URL query parameters, preserving other parameters like context, billingCycle, minChannelCount, sort, and compare.
- Trigger a reload of the plans list via getPlansForListing using the new maxMonthlyPrice filter.
- Update the UI filter control to match the new applied_filters.maxMonthlyPrice returned from getPlansForListing.

Interaction: Change minimum channel count filter
- Event: onChange on the channel count filter control (e.g., dropdown or radio group built from channel_count_options).
- Action: Update minChannelCount in the URL query parameters.
- Re-call getPlansForListing with updated filters and refresh the plans list.
- Reflect applied_filters.minChannelCount in the control state.

Interaction: Change billing cycle filter
- Event: onChange on the billing cycle control (options based on billing_cycles from getPlanFilterOptions).
- Action: Update billingCycle in the URL query parameters.
- Call getPlansForListing with the updated billingCycle filter so that only plans supporting that billing cycle (via billing_cycles_available) are returned or appropriately highlighted.
- Ensure the currently selected billingCycle is passed to startPlanChange when a plan is selected.

Interaction: Change sort order
- Event: onChange on sort control (options based on sort_options from getPlanFilterOptions).
- Action: Update sort in the URL query parameters.
- Call getPlansForListing with the new sort value while preserving filters and context.
- Re-render the plans list according to the new sort order.

Interaction: Toggle plan comparison checkbox
- Event: onClick on a comparison checkbox on a plan card.
- Action: If checked, add that plan's id to the compare list in the URL query parameter; if unchecked, remove it.
- The comparison selector reads the compare list from the URL and updates the count and availability of the Compare action.
- No backend calls are made solely on checkbox changes; comparePlans is called only when the user initiates comparison.

Interaction: Open comparison view
- Event: onClick on the Compare action in the comparison selector.
- Action: Read the current compare plan IDs from the URL parameter.
- Call comparePlans with the array of planIds.
- Render a comparison view or modal with all returned Plan data.
- The user can close the comparison view; the compare URL parameter remains unchanged.

Interaction: Clear comparison selection
- Event: onClick on a Clear or Deselect action in the comparison selector or comparison view.
- Action: Remove the compare parameter (or set it to an empty state) in the URL.
- Update the UI so that all comparison checkboxes are unchecked.
- No call to comparePlans is made unless the user re-initiates comparison.

Interaction: Select Plan
- Event: onClick on the "Select Plan" button inside a plan card.
- Action: Determine the effective billing cycle to use (from current billingCycle filter or another explicit selection control on the card or page).
- Call startPlanChange with planId (from the selected plan), billingCycle (from current filter or selection), and changeContext derived from the context URL parameter.
- If startPlanChange returns success, navigate to my_plan.html with the query parameters {from: 'plans', changePreview: 'true'}.
- If a failure is returned (success=false), show an error message in the page without navigation; the URL filters remain unchanged.

Interaction: Adjust filters or sort from billing flow
- Event: User arrives with context set to a billing-flow value (e.g., context=billing_flow) and possibly billingCycle.
- Action: Filters and sort behave exactly as in other contexts, with every change updating the URL and getPlansForListing being re-invoked.
- The page may update the URL so that context is set to the appropriate change-plan context (e.g., context=change_plan) when the user explicitly enters a change-plan sub-flow, aligning with the defined outgoing connection to plans.html with context='change_plan'.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page uses URL query parameters as the single source of truth for its state, without relying on localStorage or other client-side persistence. Incoming parameters: 'context' indicates the usage scenario (such as 'new_subscription', 'change_plan', or 'billing_flow') and is read on load to determine how to tag startPlanChange.changeContext and how to emphasize the current plan (using current_plan_id from getPlansForListing). 'billingCycle' is an optional default billing cycle filter; if provided in the URL it initializes the billing cycle filter control and is passed as filters.billingCycle to getPlansForListing. Additional filter and sort parameters defined by this page include: 'maxMonthlyPrice' (number), 'minChannelCount' (number), and 'sort' (string matching one of the sort_options values from getPlanFilterOptions). A 'compare' parameter (comma-separated list of planIds or similar URL-safe representation) is used to track which plans are selected for comparison; the page parses this parameter and keeps comparison checkboxes and the comparison selector in sync with it. On initial load, the page performs these steps: (1) parse URL parameters; (2) call getPlanFilterOptions; (3) construct a filters object using URL values constrained by getPlanFilterOptions (e.g., ensuring maxMonthlyPrice is within max_monthly_price_range); (4) call getPlansForListing with {filters, sort, context}; (5) render the page. When the user changes any filter or sort control, the page updates only the relevant URL parameter while preserving others, then re-calls getPlansForListing with the new query-derived filters and sort. When the comparison selection changes, the 'compare' parameter is updated to contain the selected planIds; this parameter is not used in getPlansForListing but is used for comparePlans. Navigations away from the page preserve state via URL. The outgoing connection to my_plan.html is triggered only after a successful startPlanChange call, and it passes 'from=plans' and 'changePreview=true' as URL parameters without including any static plan data. An outgoing self-navigation pattern is also used: when filters or sorting imply a context change into the change-plan flow, the page may update the 'context' parameter in the URL (e.g., to 'change_plan'), effectively reloading or updating the state in place.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading and re-loading on URL changes: reads URL parameters, fetches filter options, constructs filter and sort objects, and calls getPlansForListing to populate the plans list.

Manages reading and writing of URL query parameters for context, billingCycle, maxMonthlyPrice, minChannelCount, sort, and compare; exposes parsed state to other components and updates the URL when filters, sort, or comparison selection change.

Displays and manages filter controls for maximum monthly price, minimum channel count, and billing cycle using options from getPlanFilterOptions; updates the URL and triggers a plans listing refresh when any filter changes.
Provides a control to select sort order for plans using the sort_options provided by getPlanFilterOptions and updates the URL and plans listing when changed.
Renders the list of plans returned by getPlansForListing, displaying each plan via plan-card and passing down context about current_plan_id and comparison selections.

Represents a single Plan in the list, showing key details (name, description, pricing based on current billing cycle, channel_count, max_simultaneous_streams) and providing a "Select Plan" action and a comparison toggle checkbox; indicates if the plan is the current_plan_id when in a change-plan context.
Shows the number of plans currently selected for comparison based on the compare URL parameter and exposes actions to open the comparison view or clear the selection.

When opened, calls comparePlans with the planIds from the compare URL parameter and displays a side-by-side comparison of the returned Plan data, focusing on attributes like monthly_price, annual_price, channel_count, and max_simultaneous_streams.

Handles the logic for starting a plan change or new subscription: receives a planId and billingCycle from a plan-card, derives changeContext from the context URL parameter, calls startPlanChange, and performs navigation to my_plan.html on success.
Displays error messages related to failed API calls (getPlanFilterOptions, getPlansForListing, comparePlans, startPlanChange) or invalid URL parameter combinations and provides dismiss actions.

layout.grid_system: Visually consistent with a 12-column grid constrained to a centered max-width container for content; header and footer span full width with inner content aligned to this grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s simple, linear top-to-bottom flow inside a full-width dark shell. Global header and footer stay consistent with the mockup: a full-width dark header bar at the very top and a tall, multi-column footer at the bottom, both aligned to the same centered 12-column content grid. Within the Plans page content, components stack in a single main column: (1) page-level init & URL state are invisible but wrap everything; (2) page heading (contextual, not a listed component); (3) an optional full-width error/notification banner; (4) a filter/sort area as a single band; (5) the plans list (grid of plan cards) as the primary body; (6) a conditional comparison selector bar appearing near the bottom of the viewport; (7) a full-viewport comparison modal overlaying everything when active. Hidden handlers (init/state/action) live at page level with no visible footprint.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s simple, linear top-to-bottom flow inside a full-width dark shell. Global header and footer stay consistent with the mockup: a full-width dark header bar at the very top and a tall, multi-column footer at the bottom, both aligned to the same centered 12-column content grid. Within the Plans page content, components stack in a single main column: (1) page-level init & URL state are invisible but wrap everything; (2) page heading (contextual, not a listed component); (3) an optional full-width error/notification banner; (4) a filter/sort area as a single band; (5) the plans list (grid of plan cards) as the primary body; (6) a conditional comparison selector bar appearing near the bottom of the viewport; (7) a full-viewport comparison modal overlaying everything when active. Hidden handlers (init/state/action) live at page level with no visible footprint.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header remains shallow, the footer tall, and the central content has generous negative space, matching the mockup. Left-right padding for the main content container sits around 64–80px on large screens, with content constrained to a max-width centered column. Vertical spacing between major blocks (error banner, filter/sort band, plans grid, comparison selector, footer) follows the 32–48px rhythm. The plans grid occupies most of the mid-page real estate, with cards arranged in a medium-density grid. The comparison modal, when open, temporarily occupies the visual focus by covering most of the viewport (roughly 80% width, up to 80–90% height), while still leaving slim margins of the dark background visible around it.
layout_strategies.content_density: Density is tuned to medium, like the mockup. The error notification and filter/sort band are airy strips with clear padding (16–24px vertical, 16–24px horizontal). The plans grid is denser than the surrounding chrome but still breathable: consistent spacing between cards (24–32px) and comfortable card padding. The footer remains the densest area, with stacked link lists in multiple columns but adequate line height. No part of the page looks crowded or table-heavy; even the comparison view, although information-rich, uses column spacing and line height to stay readable.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup’s pattern: the eye first catches the bright CTA in the header, then the logo/navigation. From there, it drops down to the 'Plans' heading, then immediately to any high-contrast error notification strip. If no error is present, attention moves from heading directly to the filter/sort band (controls), then into the plans grid where users browse options. Within each card, bright neon CTAs draw attention. As users scroll, the comparison selector bar near the bottom of the viewport and the global floating help/call button (outside this component list but part of the shell) act as secondary attractors. If the comparison modal is opened, it takes over the flow, with a central, high-contrast panel against a dimmed background, becoming the primary focus until dismissed.

layout.overall_description: The Plans page sits inside a dark, full-width shell with a sleek, modern IPTV/gaming aesthetic. At the top, the global header spans edge to edge: logo on the left, navigation across the center, and a neon-accent CTA on the right, all aligned to an invisible 12-column grid within wide horizontal margins. Below this, the main Plans content is centered in a max-width column, starting with a large, left-aligned 'Plans' heading. Directly beneath it, when needed, a high-contrast, light notification strip stretches across the content width, clearly signaling any issues. After a comfortable vertical gap, a darker filter/sort band presents controls in a single horizontal row, maintaining the page’s calm, linear rhythm. The bulk of the page is then dominated by a responsive grid of dark plan cards, each with clean typography, neon-accent CTAs, and evenly spaced gaps to avoid visual clutter. As users select plans to compare, a dark yet prominent comparison selector bar appears near the bottom of the viewport, just above the tall multi-column footer, gently encouraging comparison. Triggering comparison brings up a wide, centered comparison modal overlay against a dimmed dark background, echoing the site’s flat, minimal style with neon highlights on key actions. Below all content, the footer mirrors the mockup with a strong brand block on the left and three link columns on the right, plus a slim bottom copyright bar. A floating circular call/help button sits anchored at the bottom-right of the viewport, independent of scroll, providing a final, subtle attractor in the user’s visual journey.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-init component acts as the invisible structural wrapper for the Plans page, occupying the entire area between the global header and footer. Visually, it defines a centered content column constrained to a max-width (e.g., ~1200–1280px) and aligned with the site-wide 12-column grid. Horizontal padding from the viewport edges is generous—around 64–80px on desktop—so the content column feels comfortably inset against the black background.
