At the bottom, there’s generous vertical space before the tall footer, which mirrors the mockup with a logo/description block on the left and three link columns. A global floating CTA button remains anchored at the bottom-right, hovering above this flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The dark header remains relatively shallow at the top. The About content area occupies the central portion of the page, using a wide but constrained content column (roughly a max-width of 1120–1200px) centered in the viewport and aligned to a 12-column grid. Within that container, the section navigation and each content section use roughly 8–10 of the 12 grid columns for readable line lengths. Vertical spacing follows the mockup’s hierarchy: about 32–48px between the title, status bar, nav, and each major section, with 16–24px internal padding for the notification/status bar. The footer occupies roughly the lower third of a long page, with its own full-width dark background, a generous top padding band, columns with 16px internal padding and 8px line spacing, and a thin copyright bar separated by an extra 24–32px of vertical space.
layout_strategies.content_density: The About page maintains a medium content density. The area around the page title, status strip, and section navigation is airy, with clear vertical breathing room similar to the header and notification bar in the mockup. Each text section uses comfortable line spacing and moderate paragraph spacing so the content feels readable, not cramped. Sections are clearly separated by 32–48px of vertical gap. The densest area is the footer, which mirrors the mockup: multiple stacked link lists in three columns plus a logo/description block on the left, but still with sufficient line height and horizontal separation to avoid clutter.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup’s pattern: the user’s eye starts at the bright CTA button in the top-right of the global header and the logo in the top-left, then travels across the main navigation. From there, attention drops down to the prominent About page heading, then immediately to the high-contrast light status/notification bar. The section navigation just below creates a horizontal focal line of section labels. After that, the eye flows downward through the stacked content sections in order—Company Overview, Mission, Timeline, Partnerships, Supported Devices—before reaching the visually heavier footer with its logo/description block and three link columns. Throughout, a small floating CTA button in the bottom-right acts as a persistent secondary attractor that sits above the scrollable content, echoing the mockup’s floating call button.

layout.overall_description: The About page is presented on a deep black background with neon green accents, matching the modern IPTV/gaming aesthetic from the mockup. At the very top, a full-width dark header spans the screen: the IPTV brand logo sits on the left, the main navigation is centered, and a bright neon green CTA (for starting or managing a subscription) anchors the right side. Below this, the content is constrained to a wide, centered container aligned to a 12-column grid, with generous horizontal margins (around 64–80px from the viewport edges on large screens). Within this container, a large, left-aligned “About” heading introduces the page, followed closely by a light-background status/notification strip that stands out sharply against the dark canvas. Just beneath, a horizontal section navigation bar lets users jump between key subsections of the About content, creating a clear index row. The main body of the page is a single vertical column of text-rich sections—Company Overview, Mission, Timeline, Partnerships, and Supported Devices—each left-aligned, spanning roughly two-thirds to three-quarters of the container width, with consistent vertical gaps between them to keep the flow calm and scannable. After the last section, generous vertical space leads into a tall, full-width dark footer: the brand logo and a short descriptive paragraph occupy the leftmost column, while three equally spaced columns labeled “Company”, “Product”, and “Help” list related links with tight but readable spacing. A thin bottom bar runs along the very bottom edge, carrying copyright and legal text. Throughout the page, a small circular neon accent button (global floating action) sits fixed at the bottom-right corner, hovering above the content and footer. The entire composition feels structured and minimal, with strong horizontals and a clean top-to-bottom narrative flow anchored by the bright accents.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-content-loader is a non-visual, structural component positioned logically at the top of the About page’s main content region, just below the global header and above the visible page title and status strip. It occupies no perceptible height or width in the visual layout, effectively behaving like an invisible controller inside the same centered max-width container as the About content. When loading is in progress, it triggers the display of the status/notification bar directly below the page heading, but the loader itself never breaks the clean vertical rhythm nor affects spacing; the surrounding components still maintain the 32–48px vertical gaps defined by the spacing system.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, non-rendered controller that sits in the normal document flow but does not consume visible space; it only influences when and how the status and content sections are shown.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-section-router is an invisible coordination layer that conceptually sits between the section navigation and the stack of content sections within the main content column. It occupies the same central column area as the content sections but has no visual footprint of its own. It aligns logically with the 12-column grid and the About content container, orchestrating which section is considered active as the user clicks on navigation items or scrolls through the page. Its presence does not introduce additional margins or padding; it simply ensures that the active state styling in the section navigation and any auto-scrolling behavior remain synchronized with the vertical flow of sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, non-visual controller with no height or width; it reacts to URL/state changes and scroll events and updates other components while preserving the linearly stacked layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-section-navigation appears as a horizontal navigation row centered within the main content container, directly beneath the About page heading and any active status/notification bar. It spans the full width of the inner content container (all 12 grid columns) but the clickable section labels themselves are left-aligned within that width, forming a row of evenly spaced text links or pill-style buttons. The row sits approximately 24–32px below the bottom edge of the status strip (or the page heading if no status is shown) and about 32px above the top of the first content section. Each navigation item has 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding, echoing the button padding from the mockup, with the currently active section highlighted by a neon green underline or text color. The nav bar is visually distinct but lightweight—text-focused with no heavy backgrounds—maintaining the minimal, flat appearance while providing a clear horizontal anchor in the otherwise vertical flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the normal document flow, aligning to the grid and scrolling off-screen with the content. It may participate in state changes (highlighting the active section) via the router and scroll manager but its physical position stays fixed relative to the surrounding sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The company-overview-section is the first major content block and sits immediately below the section navigation, separated by roughly 32–40px of vertical space. It is contained within the same centered max-width area and aligns to the 12-column grid, but the text content itself typically uses about 8–9 of those columns starting from the left, leaving a comfortable right margin for breathing room on large screens. A bold, left-aligned subsection heading (e.g., “Company Overview”) appears at the top of this block, with about 16–24px of space before the first paragraph. The body content consists of one or more paragraphs describing the IPTV provider’s background, with line lengths kept moderate and line spacing around 1.4–1.6 for readability on the dark background. The entire section is visually separated from the next with 32–48px of vertical margin below its last line of text, maintaining a calm, linear text rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level section within the main content column; it scrolls naturally with the page, maintaining its relative spacing above the mission section and below the section navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The mission-section follows directly after the company overview, with a clear vertical gap of roughly 32–40px between them. It shares the same positioning and width: located in the central content container, aligned to the left, and spanning about 8–9 grid columns so that text never feels edge-to-edge. A strong left-aligned heading (e.g., “Our Mission”) anchors the top of the section, set at the same typographic level as the Company Overview heading for consistency. Beneath this heading, one to three short paragraphs outline the IPTV service’s mission and values, each paragraph separated by 8–16px of spacing to preserve the medium-density feel. The section’s bottom margin mirrors the top (32–40px) before the start of the timeline section, preserving the linear, evenly spaced stack of sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static content block, part of the single-column vertical flow; it maintains fixed spacing relative to its neighboring sections and scrolls normally.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The timeline-section sits below the mission section with approximately 32–48px of vertical separation, occupying the same centered column but often reading slightly taller due to structured items. Its title, such as “Our Journey” or “Timeline,” is left-aligned at the top with consistent heading styling. Below the heading, key milestones are listed in chronological order, stacked vertically. Each milestone can occupy the full 8–9 grid column width and be separated by 16–24px vertically. A subtle visual device—a faint vertical line or aligned date column on the left within the section’s width—can be used to suggest a timeline without breaking the minimal flat aesthetic. The section itself may occupy roughly 1/2 to a full viewport height depending on content volume. Its bottom margin before the partnerships section remains consistent at around 32–48px, keeping the vertical rhythm intact.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level section that scrolls in sequence after the mission section; it has no special sticky behavior but works closely with the router and scroll manager for active-state tracking.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The partnerships-section appears next in the vertical flow, separated from the timeline by roughly 32–40px of space. Positioned in the same central content container, it again uses about 8–9 grid columns starting from the left, leaving an airy margin on the right. A heading such as “Partnerships” or “Trusted By” sits at the top, followed by descriptive text and/or a simple, clean list of partner names or logos. If partner logos are shown, they are arranged in a single or double row within the section’s width, spaced evenly with 16–24px gaps between them, but always on the dark background with minimal borders to keep the interface flat and modern. The section has a slightly denser feel than plain text but never becomes a heavy grid; its vertical padding ensures it still feels part of the same single-column narrative, ending with a consistent 32–40px space before the Supported Devices section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static content section; it scrolls with the main flow and remains bound to the same horizontal grid and vertical spacing rules as the other sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The supported-devices-section anchors the bottom of the About content stack, sitting about 32–48px below the partnerships section. It occupies the same central column with a left-aligned heading and uses roughly 8–10 grid columns to comfortably display device platform information. Below the heading, the content may be arranged as short descriptive text followed by a set of device categories (e.g., Smart TVs, Mobile, Desktop, Streaming Boxes) laid out either as a simple vertical list or as 2–3 columns on larger screens, but still visually perceived as part of a single overall section. Each device category is left-aligned and separated by 8–16px vertically or horizontally, keeping spacing aligned to the base 8px scale. The section’s bottom margin (32–48px) creates a clear transition into the global footer area, preserving the sense that the main About story concludes just before the dense footer content begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, final content section in the stack; it scrolls normally and establishes the terminal point of the main About content before the footer.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-scroll-manager is a non-visual behavioral layer that conceptually overlays the entire About content region—from just below the header, across the section navigation, down through all the sections. It has no visible UI, no dedicated height, and no specific visual boundary. Instead, it tracks the user’s scroll position relative to section boundaries and coordinates smooth scrolling when navigation items are clicked. It ensures that as each section’s top edge aligns within a certain threshold of the viewport, the corresponding item in the section navigation is visually highlighted, reinforcing the vertical flow without introducing any visible design elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Invisible overlay/controller bound to the scrollable area of the About page; it does not alter the layout but listens to scroll events and programmatically scrolls to section anchors when needed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-error-and-loading-state component manifests visually as a full-width notification bar inside the main content container, closely mirroring the mockup’s light notification strip. It sits directly beneath the About page heading and above the section navigation, spanning all 12 grid columns of the inner container. The bar sits on a lighter, high-contrast background (e.g., dark gray or desaturated light strip) against the black page, with rounded corners consistent with the site’s subtle rounding. Inside the bar, content is laid out horizontally: an icon or spinner on the left, followed by concise status text on the right, both aligned vertically center. Padding inside the bar is about 8–16px vertically and 16–24px horizontally, following the spacing of buttons and the original notification. When there is an error or loading state, this bar becomes a clear visual focal point; when idle, it collapses entirely so that the section navigation moves up, filling the space and preserving the overall vertical rhythm without leaving gaps.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the document flow, appearing and disappearing dynamically. When visible, it pushes the section navigation downward; when hidden, adjacent components close the gap so the column remains continuous.

name: Legal
title: Legal
description: Displays the service’s terms of use, privacy policy, and other legal notices, loaded dynamically and navigable via URL parameters.

core_features: Fetch and display the latest legal documents (terms of use, privacy policy, and other notices) using the getLegalDocuments interface.
Allow direct navigation to a specific legal section (e.g., terms, privacy, or a specific notice) via the doc URL parameter.
Provide an in-page navigation (table of contents) that lets users switch between legal sections and updates the URL state.
Support deep-linkable anchors for each legal section so that external pages can link directly to a specific legal document.
Handle browser navigation (back/forward) by reacting to URL changes and keeping the visible section in sync with the doc parameter.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Open Legal page from footer without specific section
1) User clicks the “Legal” link in the footer from any page.
2) Browser opens legal.html with no doc parameter.
3) legal-page-loader component initializes and calls getLegalDocuments once.
4) When the response returns, the component passes terms_of_use_html, privacy_policy_html, and other_notices to child components.
5) The page logic determines the default section (e.g., terms of use) when doc is missing and marks that section as active in the navigation.
6) The corresponding legal-document-section renders the HTML for the active section.
7) User can scroll through the content; other sections remain accessible via the in-page navigation.

Workflow: Open Legal page for a specific section via URL parameter
1) User follows a link such as legal.html?doc=privacy from index.html, about.html, or help.html.
2) legal-page-loader component initializes and calls getLegalDocuments.
3) After data is loaded, doc-anchor-controller reads the doc URL parameter.
4) The controller maps the doc value to the relevant section (e.g., privacy policy or a specific item in other_notices) and sets that as the active section.
5) The page scrolls to the corresponding legal-document-section (or specific notice) and highlights it in the navigation.
6) If the user uses the browser back/forward buttons and doc changes, the controller updates the active section and scrolls accordingly without re-calling getLegalDocuments.

Workflow: Switch between sections using in-page navigation
1) User is on legal.html with content already loaded via getLegalDocuments.
2) User clicks a navigation item in the legal-toc component (e.g., a tab or sidebar link representing a section or a specific notice).
3) The click handler updates the URL’s doc parameter (e.g., using history.pushState) to the corresponding section key, without reloading the page.
4) doc-anchor-controller listens to URL changes, updates the active section state, and triggers a scroll to the associated legal-document-section.
5) The newly selected section is marked active in the legal-toc, and its HTML content is visible to the user.

Workflow: Link to a specific legal notice from another page
1) On another page (index.html, about.html, or help.html), a link is constructed pointing to legal.html with an appropriate doc value representing a specific notice (e.g., a notice entry derived from other_notices).
2) User clicks that link; the browser opens legal.html?doc=<notice-key>.
3) legal-page-loader fetches legal data with getLegalDocuments.
4) doc-anchor-controller resolves the doc value to a specific item within other_notices and sets it as the active notice.
5) other-notices-list expands or scrolls to the corresponding notice item so that the user immediately sees the intended legal notice.
6) If the user navigates to a different notice via the in-page navigation, the URL doc parameter is updated accordingly, keeping the linkability intact.
interactions: On page load, legal-page-loader calls getLegalDocuments and stores the returned legal content in page state for all child components to consume.

On detection of the doc URL parameter (initial load or when it changes), doc-anchor-controller updates the active section state and scrolls into view the corresponding legal-document-section or notice item.

When the user clicks on a section in the legal-toc (e.g., terms of use, privacy policy, or a specific notice), the click handler updates the doc URL parameter via history.pushState and updates the active section without refetching data.

When the user clicks on a specific item within other-notices-list, the click handler updates the doc parameter to the identifier that represents that notice and ensures that item is expanded and scrolled into view.

When the user uses the browser back/forward buttons to navigate between different doc states, doc-anchor-controller listens to popstate events, reads the updated doc parameter, and switches the active section accordingly.

If getLegalDocuments returns an error or empty content for any of the expected fields, legal-error-handler displays a generic error or fallback message (without hardcoded legal text) and prevents rendering of incomplete sections.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state driver for this page is the doc URL parameter on legal.html. On initial load, the page reads doc from the query string (e.g., legal.html?doc=terms, legal.html?doc=privacy, or a value mapped to an element in other_notices). If doc is missing or does not map to a known section, the page selects a default section (for example, terms of use) as the active section. After getLegalDocuments completes, the doc-anchor-controller examines the current doc value, determines which section or notice it represents, and sets the active section state, then scrolls to the corresponding DOM element. When the user clicks navigation items, the page updates doc in the URL via history.pushState without reloading or re-calling getLegalDocuments; this ensures that page state (which section is active) is encoded in the URL for deep-linking and back/forward navigation. The page also listens to popstate events so that browser back/forward changes to the doc parameter are reflected in the visible section and scroll position. No persistent state is stored in localStorage or similar; all state is derived from the latest getLegalDocuments response and the current URL parameters.

components.functionality: Initializes the Legal page by calling getLegalDocuments, managing loading and error states, and distributing the returned legal document data to child components.
event_handlers: onInit: Triggered on page load to call getLegalDocuments and store the response in page-level state.
onDataLoaded: Invoked when getLegalDocuments returns successfully to notify dependent components that legal content is available.
onDataError: Invoked if getLegalDocuments fails, instructing legal-error-handler to show an appropriate failure message.

components.functionality: Synchronizes the active legal section with the doc URL parameter, handles scrolling to the correct section, and reacts to browser navigation changes.
event_handlers: onInit: Reads the initial doc parameter from the URL and sets the default active section if doc is missing or invalid.
onDocParamChange: Triggered whenever the doc parameter changes (via in-page navigation or popstate) to update the active section in page state.
onPopState: Listens to browser back/forward events, re-reading the doc parameter and updating visible content and scroll position.
onSectionActivated: Called when a section becomes active, performing a scrollIntoView for the corresponding DOM element.

components.functionality: Provides a navigational list (e.g., tabs or side navigation) of all available legal sections and notices derived from getLegalDocuments, allowing users to switch between them and updating the URL doc parameter.
event_handlers: onSectionClick: Updates URL.query.doc (using history.pushState) with the key for the clicked section and notifies doc-anchor-controller of the new active section.
onRender: Highlights the currently active section based on doc-anchor-controller.activeSectionKey.

components.functionality: Displays the terms of use HTML if available and exposes an identifiable anchor so that the doc parameter can scroll to it.
event_handlers: onMount: Registers its DOM id or anchor key with doc-anchor-controller so it can be targeted for scrolling.
onActiveChange: When doc-anchor-controller.activeSectionKey corresponds to this section, ensures the content is visible and can optionally adjust focus for accessibility.

components.functionality: Displays the privacy policy HTML and acts as the anchor target when the doc parameter indicates the privacy section.
event_handlers: onMount: Registers this section with doc-anchor-controller for scroll targeting.
onActiveChange: Responds when this section becomes active (based on doc-anchor-controller.activeSectionKey) and ensures it is scrolled into view if needed.

components.functionality: Renders the list of additional legal notices returned in other_notices, allows expanding/selecting a specific notice, and interacts with URL doc state for direct linking.
event_handlers: onNoticeClick: When a user selects a specific notice, updates URL.query.doc with the corresponding notice key and notifies doc-anchor-controller of the change.
onMount: Registers each notice item’s anchor or key with doc-anchor-controller so they can be scrolled into view when selected via doc.
onActiveChange: When the activeSectionKey matches a notice, ensures the corresponding item is expanded (if collapsible) and scrolled into view.

components.functionality: Handles and displays errors related to loading legal documents, ensuring that users receive a clear message when getLegalDocuments fails or returns incomplete data.
event_handlers: onError: Triggered when legal-page-loader encounters an error from getLegalDocuments; shows a generic error message without hardcoded legal content.
onRetryClick: If a retry option is provided, re-triggers legal-page-loader.onInit to call getLegalDocuments again.

components.functionality: Provides optional utility actions for the active legal section, such as printing the currently visible document using browser capabilities.
event_handlers: onPrintClick: Invokes window.print or equivalent browser-native printing for the current page state, allowing users to print the currently visible legal section.
onActiveSectionChange: Optionally adjusts labels or descriptions based on the activeSectionKey (e.g., to describe what will be printed) without hardcoding section names.

layout.grid_system: Visually consistent with a 12-column grid constrained to a centered max-width container for content; header and footer span full width with inner content aligned to this grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same linear, top-to-bottom structure as the mockup: full-width dark header (global), then a centered content column within a max-width container. Inside this column: a large left-aligned 'Legal' page heading (implicit), followed immediately by a full-width horizontal legal table of contents (section-navigation). Directly beneath this, transient system feedback (loader/error) appears in a light notification strip spanning the content width. Below that, the legal document sections (Terms, Privacy, Other Notices) are stacked vertically as a single reading column. The page ends with the tall, multi-column dark footer and a thin copyright bar. A separate floating global call button remains anchored in the bottom-right, above the footer and independent of content flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same linear, top-to-bottom structure as the mockup: full-width dark header (global), then a centered content column within a max-width container. Inside this column: a large left-aligned 'Legal' page heading (implicit), followed immediately by a full-width horizontal legal table of contents (section-navigation). Directly beneath this, transient system feedback (loader/error) appears in a light notification strip spanning the content width. Below that, the legal document sections (Terms, Privacy, Other Notices) are stacked vertically as a single reading column. The page ends with the tall, multi-column dark footer and a thin copyright bar. A separate floating global call button remains anchored in the bottom-right, above the footer and independent of content flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header remains a shallow band at the very top. The main legal content column occupies the central portion of the viewport, constrained to a 12-column grid within a max-width (roughly 1120–1280px) and horizontally centered with ~64–80px padding from viewport edges on large screens. Vertical spacing is generous at section boundaries: ~32–48px between the page heading, TOC, notification strip, and the first legal section; ~24–32px between subsequent sections. The legal text itself uses comfortable line height but can run long vertically, so the footer begins only after substantial scroll depth, still taking up roughly the lower quarter to third of the page once reached. Utility controls are compact, taking only a thin horizontal band above the first section, while loader/error strips occupy a single low-height band. The floating global call button sits in the bottom-right corner of the viewport with ~24–32px inset from edges.
layout_strategies.content_density: The top area (page heading and TOC) is airy, with ample whitespace around and between elements, echoing the mockup’s cart heading and notification bar spacing. The loader/error strip is visually light, with clear padding around its contents to keep it readable and non-intrusive. The legal sections themselves are the densest area, consisting of long-form text, but still broken into subsections with clear headings and ~8–16px between paragraphs. The other notices list is moderately dense, stacking links with ~8px vertical separation. Footer columns are dense but tidy, mirroring the mockup: multiple short lines of text with ~8px line spacing and ~16px column padding. Overall, density increases gradually as the user scrolls down from the minimal navigation area into the heavy legal content and footer.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye first lands on the global header’s neon CTA and logo, then drops to the left-aligned 'Legal' heading, establishing the page context. From there, attention moves horizontally across the legal TOC just beneath the heading, scanning available documents. Below that, any loader or error notification strip briefly captures attention as a high-contrast light bar against the dark background. The user then moves into a primarily vertical reading flow: Terms section first, then Privacy, then Other Notices. At the end of the scroll, the footer logo and its columns take over as the final major visual block. Throughout, a subtle cluster of print/export controls to the upper-right of the content column and the floating bottom-right call button provide secondary attractors without competing with the main reading flow.

layout.overall_description: The Legal page sits within the same dark, neon-accented shell as the rest of the IPTV marketing site. A full-width black header spans the top, with the logo on the left, centered navigation, and a bright fluorescent green CTA on the right, all aligned to a 12-column grid inside a wide, centered container. Below this, after ~32–48px of vertical breathing room, a large left-aligned 'Legal' heading appears near the top of the central content column, which is constrained to a max-width and centered on the page. Immediately beneath that heading, a low-profile, full-width horizontal legal table of contents aligns to the same grid, presenting the available legal documents as inline navigation items. Directly under this navigation, when active, a slim light notification strip (for loading states or errors) cuts across the width of the content column, creating a strong contrast with the dark background while remaining visually lightweight. The main legal body follows, forming a single, comfortable reading column: the Terms section first, then the Privacy section, then a structured list of other notices, each separated by generous vertical spacing. The text sits on the dark background but is highly legible due to high-contrast typography and a consistent line length. A compact cluster of print/export controls is tucked to the right side of the content column just above the first section, aligning with the right edge of the grid and feeling like a subtle utility bar. As the user scrolls, they eventually reach the tall dark footer, which stretches full-width but internally respects the same centered grid. The footer starts with the brand block (logo and short description) on the left and three link columns ('Company', 'Product', 'Help') filling the rest to the right, followed by a thin bottom bar with copyright. Throughout, a neon-accented floating call button hovers in the bottom-right corner of the viewport above all content, offset slightly inward from the edges and sitting visually above the footer when in view, maintaining the techy streaming vibe of the site.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-page-loader is anchored to the top of the main legal content column, conceptually sitting just below the legal TOC but above the first legal document section. When active, it manifests as a thin horizontal loading indicator or subtle shimmer bar that spans the full width of the central content column (approximately 70–80% of the viewport width on large screens), centered on the page and aligned to the 12-column grid. Vertically, it occupies a shallow band of about 24–32px height with 8–16px of padding above and below, ensuring it feels airy and does not crowd the heading or TOC. On a dark background, the loader uses a lighter strip or neon-accented progress line to be visible but not overwhelming. When inactive, it collapses and occupies no visible space, allowing the TOC to flow directly into the first legal section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-sticky, inline with content; appears and disappears without shifting the global header or footer, temporarily inserting a slim band between the TOC and the legal sections. It does not scroll independently and simply moves upward as the user scrolls down the document.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The doc-anchor-controller is a non-visual routing layer logically tied to the entire legal content column. It does not create any visible UI but controls which legal section is brought into view when URL parameters or TOC interactions change. Conceptually, it sits at the top of the main content area, governing scroll positions and focus within the Terms, Privacy, and Other Notices sections. It maintains the single-column reading flow by snapping or smoothly scrolling the viewport so that the relevant section heading aligns just below the global header and, if present, below the sticky TOC.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Occupies no physical space; behaves as an invisible controller that synchronizes scroll anchoring with the browser’s URL state. It may influence sticky behavior by ensuring target sections are positioned correctly below the header/TOC on navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-toc appears directly beneath the large 'Legal' page heading within the central content column, forming a full-width horizontal navigation strip inside the max-width container. It spans the entire width of the content column (aligned to all 12 grid columns) and is centered in the viewport with ~64–80px side padding on large screens. Individual TOC items (e.g., Terms, Privacy, Other Notices) are laid out from left to right as evenly spaced text links or pill-shaped buttons, starting at the left edge of the column. Vertically, the TOC occupies a band about 48–56px tall, with ~16px of padding above and below the links and about 24–32px of space separating it from both the page heading above and the loader/error strip below. The overall feel is minimal and flat, using the neon accent color for the active item underline or highlight. On narrower screens, the TOC may become horizontally scrollable while still occupying a single row to preserve the clean, single-column arrangement.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sticky within the content area on larger screens: once the user scrolls past it, the TOC pins just below the global header, remaining visible at the top of the viewport while the document sections scroll underneath. On small screens, stickiness may be preserved or disabled based on usability, but it always stays inline within the central column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The terms-section is the first and most prominent legal document block, positioned immediately below the loader/error strip (or directly below the TOC when no strip is visible). It spans the full width of the central content column (all 12 grid columns) but internally constrains text to a comfortable reading width, roughly equivalent to 6–8 grid columns centered within the column, to avoid very long line lengths. There is approximately 32–40px of vertical space separating its heading from the element above. The section heading is left-aligned, bold, and placed flush with the left text margin, with 16–24px of spacing before the first paragraph. Paragraphs stack vertically with ~8–12px between them, creating a dense but readable block of text against the dark background. The bottom of the terms-section is separated from the privacy-section by about 32–48px of whitespace to clearly delineate sections, preserving the mockup’s airy inter-section spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline content that scrolls naturally within the single central column. It does not stick or overlay; its vertical position relative to the viewport is governed by scrolling and the doc-anchor-controller when deep-linking to its anchor.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The privacy-section sits directly beneath the terms-section, separated by a clear vertical gap of about 32–48px to signal a new major block. It mirrors the same width and alignment behavior: full-width within the content column but with the text constrained to a 6–8 column inner width for readability, left-aligned and aligned to the same vertical text axis as the Terms section. A bold section heading appears at the top, left-aligned, with 16–24px spacing before the first paragraph. Paragraphs and any subheadings stack downward with ~8–12px vertical rhythm between lines and 16–24px above subheadings. The bottom of the privacy-section ends with similar spacing (32–48px) before transitioning into the other-notices-list, maintaining the consistent, stacked single-column layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable content in the central column. It participates in anchor-based navigation via the doc-anchor-controller, which ensures that, when targeted, its heading aligns neatly just below the sticky TOC and header without being obscured.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The other-notices-list is positioned beneath the privacy-section as the final major block of legal content before the footer. It continues the same central column alignment and width constraints, with the section heading left-aligned and aligned with the previous headings. There is about 32–40px of vertical spacing from the end of the privacy-section to this heading. Below the heading, notices are presented as a vertically stacked list of items—either short paragraphs or link rows—each separated by ~8–12px, giving a moderately dense but still readable feel. The list typically occupies the lower portion of the main content area, with the total section height varying by number of notices. A generous bottom margin of ~48–64px separates the last notice from the start of the footer, creating a clear visual transition from content to footer.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and inline within the central column, scrolling naturally. It forms the tail end of the anchor-controlled sections; selecting its entry from the TOC scrolls it into view as with the other sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-error-handler adopts the same visual pattern as the cart page’s notification strip: a full-width light bar within the central content column, positioned directly beneath the legal TOC (or below the loader if both are needed, with a small gap of 8–12px between them). It spans all 12 grid columns inside the max-width container, centered in the viewport, and is visually distinct from the dark background via a light grey or desaturated tone. The bar has ~12–16px vertical padding and ~16–24px horizontal padding on both left and right. An error icon or status indicator is positioned toward the left side, followed by concise error text aligned on a single or double line. Optional action links or a retry button sit to the right but still within the same horizontal band, wrapping if necessary on small screens. A margin of ~24–32px separates this strip from the first legal document section below, echoing the mockup’s spacing under the cart notification bar.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline and non-sticky; it appears in the normal flow of the content, pushing the legal sections downward when visible. As the user scrolls, it moves out of view like any other content block. It may temporarily replace or accompany the loader in the same vertical region beneath the TOC, but does not overlay content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-print-export-controls component is a compact horizontal cluster of utility actions positioned within the central content column, aligned to the right edge of the text grid just above the terms-section. It shares the same vertical band as the gap between the loader/error strip and the first section: for example, if there is 32px of vertical space, the controls sit in the upper part of that gap, visually anchored to the top-right corner of the document area. On large screens, it spans roughly 25–35% of the column width, with button-like controls laid out horizontally, each padded with ~8px vertical and 12–16px horizontal spacing. On smaller screens, it can wrap into a two-line cluster or center-align below the TOC while still remaining visually compact. This placement makes the controls easy to find without disrupting the primary left-aligned reading flow of the headings and body text.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Primarily inline and static, but may adopt a subtle sticky behavior within the central column on larger screens: as the user scrolls past the top of the document, the controls can gently pin just below the sticky TOC, hugging the right side of the content column until the user scrolls down far enough that it scrolls away. It never overlaps with the global floating call button, remaining higher on the page and slightly inward from the viewport edge.
